Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Lisa at 10:30 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 13:32 - Our engineers have restored power to all 1145 properties in the Wadhurst TN5 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around an underground cable fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Wadhurst TN5 area and are investigating the fault. You may find your power is going on and off; that is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've managed to restore power to a further 195 customers. There are now 84 properties still affected. We'll keep sending SMS updates until everybody is restored. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Bev at 13:08.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 15:48 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

We have been alerted to a potentially dangerous situation involving a fire close to our overhead cables, so have responded as quickly as possible and asked our control room to turn power off to the area using our high voltage network. This is why a wide area has lost electricity.

Once further specialist field staff have assessed the situation, we'll start diverting power back to affected homes as soon as they advise us it's safe. You may notice your lights going on and off as we work to get as much power restored as we can.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover 6 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This is taking place near Hollies Road and Lanham Green Road.

Logs.Comment: Good afternoon, this is a message from Harry at UK Power Networks regarding the planned power cut which is currently in progress today the 6th July 2026 in the CM77 postcode area. The power was switched off to enable our engineers to trim trees that had grown close to the overhead electricity cables near Hollies Road and Lanham Green Road. Our engineers have advised us that the 23 affected properties will be restored early between approximately 14:45 and 15:15. This is due to the works progressing well and being completed ahead of schedule. Once these works are complete this will improve the reliability of the electricity network and reduce the risk of you having a tree related unplanned power cut. Our reference number for this work is INCD-26011-O. I hope that the power coming on earlier is of benefit to you. We always aim to provide a 10/10 service, if you feel we have not delivered you a 10/10 service then please call us on 105.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.

What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 15:23 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.

Sent by Bev at 10:32 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our underground cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the East Grinstead RH19 area to investigate the power cut affecting 134 properties. 

Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable.

If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. 

For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR

Sent by Bev at 11:56 - Our engineers have restored power to all 64 properties in the Dorking Road area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. The issue was found to be on our overhead cabling network, which power has been diverted around.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 10:33 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover 7 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place near Ashen Road in Ovington.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 13:17 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Following thorough tests at our substation and on local network equipment, our staff have been able to safely replace our network equipment. The suspected underground cabling issue is not yet appearing on any of our tests, so for the moment we have restored your power as quickly as possible.

I'm afraid this may happen again, but every time you lose power it gives us a better chance of pinpointing the problem. I'm sorry for the continued disruption, but If you'd like to talk to us about this, or if your power isn't on call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We're sorry your power has gone off again. Engineers are on site to investigate what we believe to be an issue at the local substation in the St. Ann's Road area. Using an average timescale for this type of issue, we're looking to have you back on between 13:30 and 14:30, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but for more information about this type of fault; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by Lisa at 10:29.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Will at 16:52 - Just a quick check in from me as I know having a power cut is difficult, especially the impact it will be having on trying to keep cool in hotter weather. Our network is built to be resilient to all types of weather, however high temperatures like those today can impact the equipment on our network similarly to a storm or high winds. If you need any extra advice or assistance on how to stay safe in the heat, you can find the public health advice from the government website here https://UKPN.short.gy/heat. If you have any health concerns, please call NHS 111 before contacting us on 08003163105 to see how our team can assist you. Thanks again for your patience, I'll be in touch again soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 13:47 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 16:25 - We can now confirm that all power in the Dale Close area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 15:59 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Engineers have arrived on site to investigate what we believe to be an issue at the local substation in the Dale Close area. Using an average timescale for this type of issue, we're looking to have you back on between 18:30 and 19:30, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but for more information about this type of fault; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by Theo at 15:46.

Sent by Bev at 13:37 - Thanks for your patience today. Our engineers have now investigated the grounded overhead cables, and have deemed it safe to restore power back to all 129 properties in the Battle TN33 area.

We're very sorry we had to turn your power off unexpectedly, and with so little warning. We usually provide much more notice if we can, but in a situation like this our first priority is the safety of the public and our staff.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. If your power is still off or if you have any questions, call our team on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 12:51 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Battle TN33 area in the interest of the public's health and safety. This is so they can investigate reports of an overhead cable that is on the ground.

If in the area, please take care and stay at least 10m away from the cable. 

Until our engineers arrive on site and investigate, it's difficult to advise you a time frame. However, based on an average for this kind of issue, our estimated time to have you back on is between 15:30 and 16:30. 

We'll keep you updated with our progress, but in the meantime our team are always here to talk on 105 if you have any questions or concerns.
